layers.name: Training Runtime
layers.description: Python source files that implement environment dynamics, feature encoders, policies, PPO, training orchestration, telemetry, artifacts, and checkpoint handling.

layers.name: Hydra Configuration
layers.description: Hydra responsibility groups, experiment presets, sweep templates, and YAML/TOML configuration that shape training runs.

layers.name: Test Suite
layers.description: Pytest and Node test files that validate runtime behavior, config consolidation, feature parity, artifacts, and MCP bridge behavior.

layers.name: Operations and Docs
layers.description: Documentation, plans, specs, prompts, skills, scripts, and operational notes for running or guiding work on the project.

layers.name: OMG MCP Server
layers.description: TypeScript MCP server package for OMG state, PRD, memory, checkpoint, bridge, routing, and ultragoal tools.

layers.name: Miscellaneous
layers.description: Repository files that do not fit the main runtime, configuration, testing, documentation, or MCP server layers.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README and project guidance to understand Orbit Wars, the Hydra-first training workflow, and repository conventions.

tour.title: Training Entry Point
tour.description: Follow the canonical training path from the Hydra entrypoint into config validation and the JAX training loop.
tour.languageLesson: Hydra composes YAML responsibility groups before Python dataclasses and JAX code consume the resolved configuration.

tour.title: Environment and Features
tour.description: Inspect the Python and JAX environment implementations plus feature encoders that define the game state seen by policies.

tour.title: Policy and PPO
tour.description: Review model definitions, normalization, trajectory shielding, and PPO update logic that drive reinforcement learning.

tour.title: Configuration Surface
tour.description: Explore Hydra group files and experiment/sweep templates that control model capacity, task shape, rewards, training budget, opponents, telemetry, and artifacts.

tour.title: Verification and Operations
tour.description: Use the tests, documentation, and scripts to understand expected behavior, validation commands, benchmark tooling, and operational workflows.

tour.title: OMG MCP Server
tour.description: Finish with the TypeScript MCP server that supports the repository workflow tools for state, memory, PRDs, checkpoints, bridges, and ultragoals.
